message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: My feature: add transform_callback param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py. Need to touch ImageOps.py and its test file. What files does your feature touch?

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: My feature: add strip_metadata param to exif_transpose in ImageOps.py. Need to touch ImageOps.py and its test file. What files does your feature need?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: We both need exif_transpose in the same files - true overlap, can't be disjoint. Proposal: I implement+commit+push my strip_metadata param first (small, additive change to signature/docstring/body). Once I send DONE, you fetch+merge team/agent1 into your branch BEFORE adding your transform_callback param on top, so you resolve any textual overlap on your end. That way the final blind merges at submission time are clean. OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Our features both touch exif_transpose in the same file, so true disjoint ownership isn't possible. Let's both edit independently and resolve via git merge at the end. To minimize conflicts: I'll add transform_callback as a keyword-only param placed right after in_place in the signature and overloads, and only touch the method-lookup block (lines ~701-709) plus add a new test function at the end of the exif_transpose test section. Please add strip_metadata after transform_callback (or tell me your preferred order) and try to keep your new logic in a separate block/new test function too, so our diffs land in different regions where possible. Sound good?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Agreed. Go ahead and implement strip_metadata, commit+push, then send DONE. I'll fetch+merge team/agent1 first, then layer transform_callback on top and resolve any overlap myself.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
your_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Agreed: I'll add strip_metadata as a keyword-only param placed after your transform_callback param in the signature/overloads, only touch the return/save block at the end of exif_transpose (after image processing, before return), and add a new standalone test function at the end of the exif_transpose test section. Proceeding to implement now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Implemented strip_metadata param on exif_transpose: keyword-only, defaults False, placed after in_place in signature/overloads. Body change is scoped to the tail of the function (right after the existing orientation-stripping logic and in the method-is-None branches) - clears exif_image.info and exif object. Added standalone test_exif_transpose_strip_metadata at the end of the exif_transpose test section. All 35 tests in Tests/test_imageops.py pass locally. Committing and pushing now - will merge your branch before final patch.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Implemented transform_callback param on exif_transpose (added right after in_place in signature/overloads), added test_exif_transpose_transform_callback in Tests/test_imageops.py after test_exif_transpose_in_place. All 35 tests in test_imageops.py pass. Let me know when your strip_metadata change is pushed so I can merge.

my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: My strip_metadata change is now pushed to team/agent1 (commit a9d7847c3). Please fetch+merge team/agent1 into your branch if you haven't already. I'll also merge team/agent2 into mine for my final patch.txt.

note: Merged team/agent2 into agent1, resolved conflicts (kept both param sets: transform_callback before strip_metadata in signature, both docstrings, both test functions preserved). All 36 tests in test_imageops.py pass. Pushed to team/agent1. patch.txt built from team/main..HEAD contains both features.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/ImageOps.py,Tests/test_imageops.py
note: Merged your strip_metadata work into my branch, resolved conflicts (combined param order: transform_callback then strip_metadata in signature/overloads/docstring; both features compose cleanly since your logic is in the tail/elif branches and mine is in the method-lookup+transpose-loop). All 36 tests in test_imageops.py pass. Also noticed patch.txt had been committed to the repo (build artifact) - removed it from git tracking so final patch.txt generation stays clean. Final patch.txt regenerated from team/main..HEAD, confirmed it contains both transform_callback and strip_metadata changes.
